methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA creates institutional AI capacity in Richmond metro, but the partnership model signals concentrated expertise in the capital region rather than distributed capability-building across Virginia's underserved areas.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce suggests platform-driven automation is creating shallow, compliance-oriented AI adoption rather than deep technical capability development, pushing workers toward tool consumption rather than creation.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents bottom-up capacity building that could distribute AI fluency to coal-transition regions, though success depends on whether training goes beyond surface-level platform usage.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA school systems grappling with AI integration reflects the region's first-mover advantage in confronting AI pedagogy challenges, deepening the knowledge gap between well-resourced districts and rural Virginia schools without similar capacity for experimentation.

signals.title: Pittsylvania County has 2,050 jobs at risk in data center tax debate. Will a change in law cause them to disappear?
signals.signal_reading: Pittsylvania County's dependence on 2,050 data center jobs reveals infrastructure-only economic development that brings energy consumption without local AI capability development, risking both job loss and continued shallow engagement with the technology driving those facilities.
